key_insight: The paper proposes a split, structure-preserving finite element scheme for compressible resistive Hall-MHD that maintains key physical invariants while handling the fluid and magnetic dynamics with tailored solvers.
engineering_relevance: This is relevant for simulation workflows in plasma, fusion, and electromagnetic flow systems where robustness, conservation, and divergence control are critical.
method: Split operator into hydrodynamic Euler and magnetic Lorentz/induction parts
Use continuous Lagrange elements for fluid variables and curl-conforming elements for magnetic fields
Solve fluid with explicit SSP-RK and magnetic update with Crank-Nicolson plus Newton iterations
evidence_signal: The method is validated on smooth whistler waves, Orszag-Tang vortex tests, and a magnetic reconnection benchmark, showing robustness and accuracy across challenging cases.
watch_item: The magnetic solve relies on Newton iterations and an added artificial resistivity term, which may affect implementation complexity and tuning.
paper_title: A structure-preserving Numerical Method for the Compressible Resistive-Hall-MHD System
